description_long: This structure is a large, traditional Japanese pagoda with multiple stacked, tiled roofs and a stone foundation. The building features several interconnected sections, each with its own tiered roof, and the central tower rises above the rest with a prominent, ornate gable. The roofs are covered in blue-gray tiles with decorative ridge elements, and the walls are primarily dark wood with some lighter stonework at the base. The windows and doors are rectangular and symmetrically arranged, with wooden latticework visible in some areas. The overall design is characterized by its multi-level, sprawling layout and the harmonious blend of stone, wood, and tile materials. The structure is imposing and would serve as a landmark or centerpiece in an urban environment.
description: A large, multi-tiered Japanese pagoda with blue-gray tiled roofs, dark wooden walls, and a stone foundation. The structure features interconnected sections and a prominent central tower.
description_view_0: The front-left view highlights the sprawling layout, showing the central tower and multiple roof tiers extending to the right.
description_view_1: The direct front view emphasizes the symmetry of the central tower and the layered roofs leading up to it.
description_view_2: The rear-left view displays the interconnected sections and the stone base, with the central tower rising above.
description_view_3: The direct rear view focuses on the back of the central tower and the alignment of the roof tiers.
